name: ai_agent_ha
description: A Home Assistant custom component that provides an AI-powered agent capable of generating automations based on natural language queries. The agent connects to all entities in your Home Assistant instance and uses OpenAI's or Llama API to translate user requests into valid Home operations including creating automations for you!

Fix deprecated Lovelace and recorder history APIs for HA 2026.2+

- Update Lovelace dashboard access to use property-based API
  (lovelace_data.dashboards instead of dictionary access)
- Update history import to use recorder.history module
- Remove plan file from PR
- Apply black formatting

Closes #38

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix deprecated Lovelace API access (Issue #38)

Replace dictionary-style access with property access for Home Assistant's
Lovelace component data, which will be required in HA 2026.2+.

Changes:
- get_dashboards(): Use lovelace_data.dashboards property instead of
  lovelace_config.get(CONF_DASHBOARDS, {})
- get_dashboards(): Get metadata (title, icon) from yaml_dashboards
  instead of incorrectly accessing LovelaceConfig.config
- get_dashboard_config(): Use dashboards dict with None key for default
  dashboard instead of lovelace_config.get("default_dashboard")
- Remove unused imports (lovelace_ws, require_admin) and MockConnection class
- Add hasattr() checks for backward compatibility with older HA versions
- Remove deprecated CONF_DASHBOARDS import

The LovelaceData dataclass stores dashboards as dict[str | None, LovelaceConfig]
where None represents the default dashboard. Dashboard metadata (title, icon,
show_in_sidebar, require_admin) is stored in yaml_dashboards.

Fixes #38

Fix deprecated Lovelace API access (Issue #38)

Replace dictionary-style access with property access for Home Assistant's
Lovelace component data, which will be required in HA 2026.2+.

Changes:
- get_dashboards(): Use lovelace_data.dashboards property instead of
  lovelace_config.get(CONF_DASHBOARDS, {})
- get_dashboards(): Get metadata (title, icon) from yaml_dashboards
  instead of incorrectly accessing LovelaceConfig.config
- get_dashboard_config(): Use dashboards dict with None key for default
  dashboard instead of lovelace_config.get("default_dashboard")
- Remove unused imports (lovelace_ws, require_admin) and MockConnection class
- Add hasattr() checks for backward compatibility with older HA versions
- Remove deprecated CONF_DASHBOARDS import

The LovelaceData dataclass stores dashboards as dict[str | None, LovelaceConfig]
where None represents the default dashboard. Dashboard metadata (title, icon,
show_in_sidebar, require_admin) is stored in yaml_dashboards.

Fixes #38

Add plan for fixing deprecated Lovelace API access (Issue #38)

Document the fix needed for dictionary-style Lovelace data access
that will be deprecated in Home Assistant 2026.2. The plan outlines
changes to get_dashboards() and get_dashboard_config() methods to
use property-based access instead.

commit.message:
Remove broken logbook functionality

The get_logbook_entries feature was using a non-existent
logbook.get_events API that doesn't exist in Home Assistant 2025.11+.
This caused errors when the AI tried to answer questions about
recent events/activity.

Fixes #35

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Alter provider not recognized in process_query and frontend

- Add Alter to provider_config dictionary in process_query method
  (this was causing "Invalid provider alter, falling back to llama" error)
- Add Alter to PROVIDERS constant in frontend panel JavaScript
- Add Alter to titleToProviderMap in frontend panel JavaScript

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Alter API support

Adds support for Alter (https://alterhq.com) as a new AI provider.
Alter is an OpenAI-compatible API router similar to OpenRouter.

Changes:
- Add AlterClient class in agent.py with OpenAI-compatible implementation
- Add Alter to provider configuration in config_flow.py
- Add CONF_ALTER_TOKEN constant and "alter" to AI_PROVIDERS list
- Add Alter provider validation in __init__.py
- Add UI strings for Alter in translations/en.json and strings.json
- Fix duplicate "Custom..." option in model dropdown for providers
  that only have custom model entry

Configuration:
- Endpoint: https://alterhq.com/api/v1/chat/completions
- Authentication: Bearer token
- Model: User-specified (no predefined list)

Closes #33

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix area information retrieval in get_entity_registry method

- Resolve area_id from device when entity has no direct area assignment
- Add area_name field to entity registry results
- Matches the logic used in get_entity_state for consistency

This fixes the issue where entities without direct area assignments
(like Zigbee sensors that inherit areas from devices) were showing
"No Area" when queried in bulk via get_entity_registry.

Add comprehensive debug logging for area information retrieval

- Log when entity is found/not found in registry
- Log when entity has direct area assignment
- Log when checking device area assignment
- Log when area_id is resolved to area_name
- Change exception logging from DEBUG to WARNING level
- Add area_id and area_name to final entity state log

This will help diagnose why users see "No Area" for entities
that should have areas assigned in Home Assistant.

Add additional safety checks for registry method availability

- Check for hasattr before calling async_get methods
- Catch all exceptions to prevent test failures
- Ensures backward compatibility with mocked environments

Make area information retrieval more defensive in get_entity_state

- Wrap registry access in try-except to handle test environments
- Add hasattr checks before accessing registry attributes
- Gracefully handle cases where registries aren't available
- Fixes KeyError issues in test suite

This ensures the code works both in production (with full Home Assistant)
and in test environments (with mocked components).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix import sorting in get_entity_state method

- Alphabetize imports (area_registry, device_registry, entity_registry)
- Resolves isort CI check failure

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Gemini MAX_TOKENS issue and remove token limits across all providers

This commit addresses issue #30 where Gemini 2.5 models were hitting MAX_TOKENS limits due to extended thinking tokens consuming the output budget.

Changes:
- Remove max_tokens/maxOutputTokens limits from all optional providers (OpenAI, Gemini, OpenRouter, Llama, Local)
- Keep max_tokens for Anthropic (required parameter, increased to 8192)
- Add enhanced logging for Gemini token usage and finish reasons
- Remove unused _get_token_parameter() method from OpenAI client
- Allow each provider to use its model's maximum capacity by default

Benefits:
- Fixes MAX_TOKENS truncation in Gemini 2.5 with extended thinking
- No artificial limits on response length
- Simpler configuration with better defaults
- Complete dashboard generation responses

Version bumped to 1.01

Fix Home Assistant 2025.12 deprecation warning for OptionsFlow

Updated the OptionsFlow implementation to use the new pattern that
eliminates manually setting config_entry. The framework now
automatically provides the config_entry property.

Changes:
- Remove config_entry parameter from AiAgentHaOptionsFlowHandler.__init__()
- Update async_get_options_flow() to instantiate handler without parameters
- Remove manual self.config_entry assignment

This ensures compatibility with Home Assistant 2025.12 and later versions.

Fix GitHub Actions release permissions issue

Add contents: write permission to the release job to allow creating GitHub releases. The default GITHUB_TOKEN has restricted permissions and needs explicit grant for release creation.

Fixes #2

Fix test mocks to include last_changed and friendly_name attributes

- Added last_changed = None to all mock state objects
- Added friendly_name to attributes for all mock entities
- Ensures get_entity_state() can properly serialize mock states
- Fixes 4 failing tests in GitHub Actions CI

Tests fixed:
- test_get_entities_by_device_class
- test_get_climate_related_entities
- test_get_climate_related_entities_sensors_only
- test_climate_related_entities_deduplication

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix critical Anthropic API bug and add climate dashboard support

CRITICAL FIX:
- Fixed system prompt being overwritten by data payloads in Anthropic API
  - Changed data responses from "role": "system" to "role": "user"
  - Prevents Claude from ignoring formatting instructions
  - Resolves issue where Claude returned YAML in final_response instead of JSON with dashboard_suggestion
  - Affects custom_components/ai_agent_ha/agent.py lines ~2738, ~2854, ~2995

CLIMATE DASHBOARD ENHANCEMENTS:
- Added get_entities_by_device_class(device_class, domain) function
  - Filters entities by device_class attribute (temperature, humidity, motion, etc.)
  - Optional domain filter for performance optimization
- Added get_climate_related_entities() function
  - Combines climate.* entities with temperature/humidity sensors
  - Automatic deduplication to prevent duplicate entities
  - Resolves GitHub issue where users with only temp/humidity sensors couldn't create climate dashboards
- Enhanced dashboard templates with device_class support
  - History graphs for temperature and humidity visualization
  - Entity cards showing current sensor values
  - Properly categorized sensor groups by device_class
- Enhanced get_entity_registry() to include device_class, state_class, unit_of_measurement

ANTHROPIC PROVIDER UPDATES:
- Updated default Anthropic model to Claude Sonnet 4.5 (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929)
- Added claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 to available models list

SYSTEM PROMPT IMPROVEMENTS:
- Added device_class guidance for improved AI understanding
- Enhanced dashboard creation instructions
- Updated both SYSTEM_PROMPT and SYSTEM_PROMPT_LOCAL consistently

TESTING:
- Added unit tests for get_entities_by_device_class()
- Added unit tests for get_climate_related_entities()
- Added unit tests for deduplication logic
- Added critical test verifying data payloads use 'user' role not 'system'
- Enhanced dashboard template tests for climate sensor handling

DOCUMENTATION:
- Comprehensive changelog entry documenting all changes
- Updated code comments explaining critical fixes

Files modified:
- custom_components/ai_agent_ha/agent.py
- custom_components/ai_agent_ha/config_flow.py
- custom_components/ai_agent_ha/dashboard_templates.py
- tests/test_ai_agent_ha/test_agent.py
- tests/test_ai_agent_ha/test_dashboard_templates.py
- CHANGELOG.md
- custom_components/ai_agent_ha/manifest.json (version bump)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add comprehensive troubleshooting docs and fix critical security issue

## Security Fixes
- **CRITICAL**: Fix API key exposure in debug logs (agent.py:2217)
  - Previously logged entire config dict with all API tokens in plaintext
  - Now sanitizes all sensitive data before logging
- Add sanitize_for_logging() utility function
  - Recursively masks API keys, tokens, passwords, secrets
  - Handles nested dicts, lists, tuples
  - Case-insensitive pattern matching
- Sanitize response headers to prevent auth token leakage
- All sensitive fields now masked as ***REDACTED*** in logs

## New Features
- Add comprehensive troubleshooting guide (docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md)
  - Step-by-step debug log enablement instructions
  - Two methods for collecting logs (UI and file-based)
  - Solutions for 10+ common issues including climate vs sensor confusion
  - Detailed issue reporting guide with template
- Add 16 comprehensive tests for sanitization function
  - Tests cover tokens, API keys, passwords, headers
  - Validates nested structures and case-insensitivity
  - All tests passing

## Documentation Updates
- Update README.md with link to troubleshooting guide
- Enhance bug report template with log collection instructions
- Update documentation to reflect auto-sanitization of API keys
  - Users no longer need to manually redact keys from logs
  - Simplified log sharing process

## Testing
- Flake8: No linting errors ✓
- MyPy: No type errors ✓
- Bandit: No security issues (3657 lines scanned) ✓
- Pytest: 16/16 sanitization tests passing ✓

commit.message:
Fix Gemini API 404 errors by updating to Gemini 2.x models

Resolves #25 by replacing retired Gemini 1.x models with current 2.x models.

## Changes:
- Updated default Gemini model from gemini-1.5-flash to gemini-2.5-flash
- Replaced retired models (gemini-1.0-pro, gemini-1.5-flash, gemini-1.5-pro)
  with current models (gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.0-flash)
- Added preview models (gemini-2.5-flash-preview, gemini-2.5-pro-preview)
- Kept v1beta endpoint as per Google's current API documentation
- Bumped version from 0.99.6 to 0.99.7

## Root Cause:
Google retired Gemini 1.0 models in April 2025 and Gemini 1.5 models in
September 2025. The 404 errors occurred because the integration was using
model names that no longer exist.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

releases.name: v1.07
releases.body:
Fixes for Home Assistant 2026.2+ Compatibility

  1. Lovelace Dashboard API Migration (Issue #38)
  - Fixed deprecated dictionary-style access to Lovelace dashboard data
  - Changed from lovelace_data['dashboards'] to lovelace_data.dashboards (property access)
  - Updated both get_dashboards() and get_dashboard_config() methods
  - Added backward compatibility checks with hasattr() guards

  2. Recorder History API Update
  - Migrated from deprecated homeassistant.components.history module
  - Now uses homeassistant.components.recorder.history.get_significant_states
  - Ensures history queries continue working in future HA versions

  3. Code Quality Improvements
  - Added type narrowing for mypy compatibility
  - Removed unused imports and dead code (MockConnection class, unused variables)
  - Applied black formatting

  Files Modified

  - custom_components/ai_agent_ha/agent.py
  - custom_components/ai_agent_ha/manifest.json

readme_html:
AI Agent HA

A powerful Home Assistant custom integration that connects your Home Assistant instance with multiple AI providers (OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic (Claude), OpenRouter, Alter, z.ai, and Llama) to translate user requests into valid Home Assistant operations, including creating automations automatically!

🚀 Quick Install

Click the button above to install AI Agent HA directly through HACS, or see the detailed installation instructions below.

☕ Support the Project

If you find this integration helpful and would like to support its development, you can buy me a coffee! Your support helps keep this project active and maintained. Currently I am the only Dev on that project and looking to have more paretners here.

Every contribution, no matter how small, is greatly appreciated and helps fund the continued development and improvement of AI Agent HA.

Another way to support me will be to try my new project. Askie - AI for kids. I am looking for feedback on this one: ⁠Web: https://kidsai.app • ⁠iOS: https://apps.apple.com/app/id6749299565 ⁠Android: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.askie.app

✨ Features

  • 🤖 Multiple AI Provider Support: OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic (Claude), OpenRouter, Alter, z.ai, and Llama
  • 🎯 Model Selection: Choose from predefined models or use custom model names
  • 🏠 Smart Home Control: Turn lights on/off, control climate, and manage devices
  • ⚡ Automation Creation: Automatically create automations based on natural language
  • 📋 Dashboard Creation: Create and customize Home Assistant dashboards through natural language
  • 📊 Data Access: Get entity states, history, weather, and more
  • 🔒 Secure: API keys stored securely in Home Assistant
  • 🎨 Beautiful UI: Clean, modern chat interface
  • 🔄 Real-time: Instant responses and updates

📸 Screenshots

Automation Creation

📋 Dashboard Creation

AI Agent HA now supports creating and managing Home Assistant dashboards through natural language conversations! Simply describe what you want, and the AI will create a complete dashboard for you.

How Dashboard Creation Works

  1. Natural Language Request: Ask the AI to create a dashboard for any purpose
  2. Entity Discovery: The AI automatically finds relevant entities in your Home Assistant
  3. Smart Organization: Entities are organized by room, functionality, or domain
  4. Dashboard Generation: Complete dashboard with proper cards and layout is created
  5. Integration: Dashboard is automatically added to your Home Assistant sidebar
  6. Restart Required: You'll need to restart Home Assistant to see the new dashboard in your sidebar

Dashboard Creation Examples

Simple Room Dashboard

"Create a dashboard for my living room lights"

The AI will find all living room light entities and create a dashboard with appropriate light control cards.

Security Dashboard

"Create a security dashboard with all door sensors, cameras, and alarm controls"

The AI will create a comprehensive security monitoring dashboard with sensor states, camera feeds, and alarm controls. After creation, restart Home Assistant to see the new dashboard in your sidebar.

Energy Monitoring Dashboard

"I want an energy dashboard showing power consumption and usage graphs"

The AI will create an energy monitoring dashboard with real-time power gauges, usage graphs, and cost tracking.

Climate Control Dashboard

"Create a climate dashboard for temperature control throughout the house"

The AI will organize thermostats, temperature sensors, and HVAC controls in a logical layout.

Supported Dashboard Features

  • Smart Card Selection: Appropriate card types for each entity (lights, sensors, media players, etc.)
  • Room-Based Organization: Entities automatically grouped by area when possible
  • Interactive Clarification: AI asks follow-up questions to refine your requirements
  • Template-Based Creation: Built-in templates for common dashboard types (security, energy, climate, etc.)
  • Dynamic Layout: Optimized card arrangements and view organization
  • Icon Integration: Automatic Material Design icon selection

Dashboard Types the AI Can Create

  • Room-Specific: Living room, bedroom, kitchen, etc.
  • Functional: Security, energy, climate, media, lighting
  • Device-Specific: All lights, all sensors, all switches
  • Scenario-Based: Morning routine, evening security, vacation mode
  • Custom: Any combination based on your specific needs

For detailed dashboard creation documentation, see: Dashboard Creation Guide

🚀 Supported AI Providers

OpenAI

  • Models: GPT-3.5 Turbo, GPT-4, GPT-4 Turbo, GPT-4o, GPT-5, O1-Preview, O1-Mini
  • Setup: Get API key from OpenAI Platform

Google Gemini

  • Models: Gemini 1.5 Flash, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Gemini 1.0 Pro, Gemini 2.0 Flash Exp
  • Setup: Get API key from Google AI Studio

Anthropic (Claude)

  • Models: Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3.5 Haiku, Claude 3 Opus, Claude 3 Sonnet, Claude 3 Haiku
  • Setup: Get API key from Anthropic Console
  • Popular Models:
    • claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 (Latest and most capable)
    • claude-3-5-haiku-20241022 (Fast and efficient)
    • claude-3-opus-20240229 (Most powerful for complex tasks)

OpenRouter

  • Models: Access to 100+ models including Claude, Llama, Mistral, and more
  • Setup: Get API key from OpenRouter
  • Popular Models:
    • anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet
    • meta-llama/llama-3.1-70b-instruct
    • mistralai/mixtral-8x7b-instruct

Llama

  • Models: Llama 4 Maverick, Llama 3.1, Llama 3.2
  • Setup: Get API key from your Llama provider

Alter

  • Models: Various custom models
  • Setup: Get API key from Alter HQ

z.ai

  • Models: GLM-4.7, GLM-4.6, GLM-4.5, GLM-4.5-air, GLM-4.5-x, GLM-4.5-airx, GLM-4.5-flash
  • Setup: Get API key from Z.ai Platform
  • Endpoints:
    • General Purpose: Standard AI tasks
    • Coding: Optimized for coding scenarios (3× usage, 1/7 cost)
  • Popular Models:
    • glm-4.7 (Latest flagship model)
    • glm-4.5-flash (Fast and efficient)

📦 Installation

HACS Installation (Recommended)

Use the Quick Install button at the top of this README for the easiest installation, or manually add the repository:

  1. Open HACS in your Home Assistant instance
  2. Click on "Integrations"
  3. Click the three dots in the top right corner
  4. Select "Custom repositories"
  5. Add this repository: https://github.com/sbenodiz/ai_agent_ha
  6. Select "Integration" as the category
  7. Click "Add"
  8. Find "AI Agent HA" in the integration list
  9. Click "Download"
  10. Restart Home Assistant
  11. Go to Settings → Devices & Services → Add Integration
  12. Search for "AI Agent HA"
  13. Follow the setup wizard to configure your preferred AI provider

Manual Installation

  1. Download the latest release from the releases page
  2. Extract the files
  3. Copy the custom_components/ai_agent_ha folder to your Home Assistant custom_components directory
  4. Restart Home Assistant
  5. Go to Settings → Devices & Services → Add Integration
  6. Search for "AI Agent HA"
  7. Follow the setup wizard to configure your preferred AI provider

⚙️ Configuration

The integration uses a two-step configuration process:

Step 1: Choose AI Provider

Select your preferred AI provider from the dropdown:

  • OpenAI
  • Google Gemini
  • Anthropic (Claude)
  • OpenRouter
  • Alter
  • z.ai
  • Llama
  • Local Model

Step 2: Configure Provider

Enter your API credentials and optionally select a model:

  • API Key/Token: Your provider-specific API key
  • Model: Choose from predefined models or enter a custom model name

Configuration Examples

# Example configuration.yaml (optional - integration supports config flow only)
ai_agent_ha:
  ai_provider: anthropic
  anthropic_token: "sk-ant-..."
  models:
    anthropic: "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022"

# Example with z.ai provider
ai_agent_ha:
  ai_provider: zai
  zai_token: "your-zai-api-key"
  zai_endpoint: "general"  # or "coding" for coding-optimized endpoint
  models:
    zai: "glm-4.7"

# Example with Alter provider
ai_agent_ha:
  ai_provider: alter
  alter_token: "your-alter-api-key"
  models:
    alter: "your-model-name"

🎮 Usage

Chat Interface

Access the beautiful chat interface at:

  • Sidebar: AI Agent HA panel
  • URL: http://your-ha-instance:8123/ai_agent_ha

🔧 Advanced Features

Custom Models

Enter any model name in the "Custom Model" field:

  • OpenAI: gpt-4-0125-preview
  • Anthropic: claude-3-opus-20240229
  • OpenRouter: anthropic/claude-3-opus
  • Gemini: gemini-pro-vision
  • Alter: your-custom-model-name
  • z.ai: glm-4.7
  • Llama: Llama-4-Maverick-17B-128E-Instruct-FP8

Automation Creation

The AI can create automations automatically:

  1. Ask: "Create an automation to turn on lights at sunset"
  2. Review the generated automation
  3. Approve or reject the suggestion
  4. Automation is added to your Home Assistant

Dashboard Creation

The AI can create custom dashboards through conversation:

  1. Ask: "Create a security dashboard with cameras and sensors"
  2. AI discovers relevant entities and asks clarifying questions
  3. Dashboard is generated with appropriate cards and layout
  4. Dashboard is automatically added to your Home Assistant sidebar
  5. Restart Home Assistant to see the new dashboard

Data Access

The AI can access comprehensive Home Assistant data:

  • Entity states and history
  • Weather information
  • Person locations
  • Device registry
  • Area/room information
  • Statistics and analytics

🛠️ Development

Contributing

We welcome contributions from the community! Whether you're fixing bugs, adding features, or improving documentation, your help is appreciated.

Please check out our contribution guidelines for detailed information on how to contribute to this project.

Quick Start

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch
  3. Make your changes
  4. Test thoroughly
  5. Submit a pull request

For security issues, please review our security policy.

Local Frontend Testing

You can test the frontend UI locally without deploying to Home Assistant:

  1. Start the local test server:

    cd tests/frontend
python3 serve.py

  2. Open your browser: Navigate to http://localhost:8000/test_panel.html

  3. Test the interface: The test page provides a mock Home Assistant environment where you can test UI changes, styling, and component behavior without needing a full Home Assistant installation.

Test files location:

  • tests/frontend/test_panel.html - Standalone test page
  • tests/frontend/serve.py - Simple HTTP server for testing

This is particularly useful for:

  • Testing CSS changes and styling
  • Verifying UI layout and responsiveness
  • Debugging frontend JavaScript issues
  • Quick iteration on visual changes

CI/CD Workflows

This project uses GitHub Actions to ensure code quality and reliability:

  • Quality Checks: Runs all checks in a single workflow (Python 3.12)
  • Python Linting: Checks code style with flake8, black, and isort
  • Python Type Checking: Verifies typing with mypy (Python 3.12 compatibility)
  • Python Tests: Runs tests with pytest (Python 3.12)
  • Security Scan: Checks for security vulnerabilities with Bandit
  • Home Assistant Validation: Validates the integration with hassfest
  • HACS Validation: Ensures compatibility with HACS

All workflows run automatically on push and pull requests using Python 3.12 to ensure compatibility with the latest Home Assistant versions. You can also run them manually via the "Actions" tab in the GitHub repository.

API Structure

The integration provides these main components:

  • AI Clients: Modular providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic (Claude), OpenRouter, Alter, z.ai, Llama, Local)
  • Agent: Core logic for processing requests
  • Config Flow: Setup and options management
  • Frontend: Chat interface
  • Dashboard Templates: Templates for dashboard creation

📋 Requirements

  • Home Assistant 2023.3+
  • Python 3.12+ (required for compatibility with Home Assistant 2025.1.x+)
  • One of the supported AI provider API keys

Python Version Note

Important: Starting with version 0.99.3, this integration requires Python 3.12 or later. This change was made to ensure compatibility with Home Assistant 2025.1.x and later versions, which use syntax features only available in Python 3.12+.

If you're running an older Home Assistant version with Python 3.11, please use version 0.99.2 or earlier of this integration.

🔒 Security

  • API keys are stored securely in Home Assistant's encrypted storage
  • All communication uses HTTPS
  • No data is stored outside your Home Assistant instance
  • Provider-specific security practices are followed

📝 License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the LICENSE file for details.

🆘 Support

  • Troubleshooting: Troubleshooting Guide - Common issues and solutions
  • Issues: GitHub Issues
  • Discussions: GitHub Discussions
  • Documentation: Wiki

🙏 Acknowledgments

  • Home Assistant community for the excellent platform
  • All AI providers for their powerful APIs
  • Special thanks to @RmG152 for their valuable help with development
  • Contributors and testers who help improve this integration

Made with ❤️ for the Home Assistant community
